methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
EnerVenue, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Yi Cui, is evaluated against a comparison universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) in the Climate & Energy sector at identical stage. The peer set spans Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others, establishing a valuation range from $120M to $5.0B with a median of $355M.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's $400M total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $760M. Peer-median methodology, anchored to the $355M median across six same-sector, same-stage comparables, suggests $355M. These methods produced a low bound of $302M and high bound of $874M, with a triangulated median of $558M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M–$874M, centered near $558M. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B), the use of total capital raised as a proxy absent explicit post-money valuation data, and the representativeness of the peer cohort for a capital-intensive frontier technology company at Series B maturity.
